question.label: High-ambiguity forums
question.name: High-ambiguity forums — what makes those rooms feel risky for you?
question.description: Drills into the specific avoidance pattern to surface the real constraint behind stalled stretch.

answer: I think I need to own a cross-org decision area, not just deliver strong design outcomes.

assessment.note: He names a concrete next-level aspiration — owning a cross-org decision area — which signals strong growth direction and some continued investment.

new_queue.label: Cross-org decisions
new_queue.name: Cross-org decision area — which decisions do you want your name attached to?

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Sponsorship need
new_queue.name: What kind of sponsorship would actually change the way your work lands here?

new_queue.label: Next two weeks
new_queue.name: Given what we've talked through, what would you want to focus on first over the next two weeks?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: Sometimes I avoid high-ambiguity forums because I do not want to sound underprepared.
I think I need to own a cross-org decision area, not just deliver strong design outcomes.
I am still motivated, but the promotion outcome made me more careful about where I spend political capital.
